root_text.ud2.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud2.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā kumārakā antarā ca sāvatthiṁ antarā ca jetavanaṁ ahiṁ daṇḍena hananti.
root_text.ud2.3:1.4: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.ud2.3:1.5: Addasā kho bhagavā sambahule kumārake antarā ca sāvatthiṁ antarā ca jetavanaṁ ahiṁ daṇḍena hanante.
root_text.ud2.3:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud2.3:1.3: Now at that time, between Sāvatthī and the Jeta Grove, several boys were hitting a snake with a stick.
translation_text.ud2.3:1.4: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud2.3:1.5: He saw the boys hitting the snake.
translation_text.ud2.3:2.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.3:3.2: so if you harm them with a stick
translation_text.ud2.3:3.3: in search of your own happiness,
translation_text.ud2.3:3.4: after death you’ll find no happiness.
translation_text.ud2.3:4.2: so if you don’t harm them with a stick
translation_text.ud2.3:4.3: in search of your own happiness,
translation_text.ud2.3:4.4: after death you will find happiness.”
